worth_it_explanation: DV Pinnacle is the reporting and management layer that ties together every measurement signal DoubleVerify collects. When Pinnacle is structured correctly, viewability, fraud, and brand safety data roll up at the campaign and channel level in a way that is actionable. When it is not, practitioners are looking at aggregate numbers they cannot diagnose or act on. Fixing the setup is a one-time configuration effort, and the payoff is measurement data that reflects the actual media plan for the life of the account.
intro:
DoubleVerify (DV) is a third-party ad verification and measurement vendor. Its platform, DV Pinnacle, is where advertisers and agencies manage their measurement settings, monitor campaign quality, and access viewability, fraud, and brand safety data across their media buys. Pinnacle receives impression-level data from demand-side platforms (DSPs), ad servers, and direct publisher integrations, and organizes it by the campaign structure the practitioner sets up.

For that data to be useful, three things need to be in place. First, Pinnacle must be structured to mirror the media plan, so reporting can be segmented by campaign, channel, or partner. Second, DV measurement must be deployed on all active media, so a share of impressions is not left unmeasured. Third, the data connections from buying platforms need to be correctly configured, so what Pinnacle reports reflects what is actually running. These checks assess each of those conditions.

audit_checks.title: Pinnacle is not structured to mirror the media plan, so reporting cannot be segmented by campaign or channel
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When the campaign structure in Pinnacle does not match the media plan, viewability, fraud, and brand safety data can only be read at an aggregate level. A practitioner cannot determine whether a quality problem is isolated to one channel, one DSP, or one campaign without an organizing structure that reflects the actual buy. Diagnosing or acting on the data requires re-pulling and manually segmenting it, and some issues may go unaddressed because they are invisible in the rolled-up view.

educate.base:
DV Pinnacle organizes measurement data by a campaign hierarchy that the advertiser or agency configures. That hierarchy is how Pinnacle knows which impressions belong to which campaign, which flight, and which channel. When the hierarchy is set up with names and groupings that do not correspond to the actual media plan, the data in Pinnacle cannot be filtered or segmented to match the way the account is bought and reported on.

This is a structural problem, not a data-quality problem. DV is measuring correctly, but the results are only visible at a level of aggregation that is too broad to act on. A brand running programmatic display, paid social, and video as separate budget lines may see a single blended quality score in Pinnacle that does not tell it whether the viewability problem is in programmatic or video, or whether the fraud signal is coming from one DSP or from across the whole plan.

The campaign structure in Pinnacle should reflect the dimensions the account team cares about for optimization and reporting. If the media plan is organized by channel, the Pinnacle structure should separate channels. If it is organized by campaign or brand, those should map to distinct Pinnacle campaigns. The naming conventions used in the DSP or ad server are a reasonable starting point. Pinnacle can be configured to receive data at a campaign-ID level from most integrated buying platforms.

A few signs suggest the structure needs review:
  • Pinnacle shows a single campaign or a small number of campaigns that aggregate across multiple DSPs or channels
  • The campaign names in Pinnacle do not match the naming convention used in the media plan, the DSP, or the agency's reporting system
  • Practitioners are unable to filter Pinnacle data to a specific campaign or channel without exporting and re-cutting it
You may want to compare the campaign list in Pinnacle against the current media plan to confirm each line item or channel has a corresponding Pinnacle campaign with a recognizable name.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retailer running campaigns across brand search, prospecting display, and retargeting, each of those lines typically has a different quality profile and a different tolerance for viewability and fraud risk. You may want to confirm that each line has its own Pinnacle campaign so quality data can be evaluated against the right benchmark.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser with campaigns segmented by service line or geography, Pinnacle campaigns that mirror those segments make it possible to see whether a quality issue is concentrated in a specific region or product area. You may want to confirm that the Pinnacle structure reflects those segments rather than combining all lead-gen activity into a single campaign.

educate.learn_more_note: Authoritative guidance on creating and organizing campaigns in DV Pinnacle is available in the DoubleVerify Pinnacle knowledge base, which requires a Pinnacle login. Contact your DoubleVerify account team for setup documentation.

investigate.intro: Compare the campaign list in Pinnacle against the current media plan to assess whether the structure supports channel- or campaign-level segmentation.

dv_pinnacle.label: DoubleVerify Pinnacle
steps: Log in to DV Pinnacle and open the campaign list view. Note how many distinct campaigns are configured and what naming convention they use.

Pull up the current media plan or the DSP campaign list and compare it against the Pinnacle campaign list. For each active media line, confirm there is a corresponding Pinnacle campaign with a recognizable name.
For any Pinnacle campaign whose name does not match a media plan line or DSP campaign, open its settings and review which advertiser it belongs to and which DSPs or data connections are feeding it.
Attempt to filter the Pinnacle reporting dashboard to a single campaign or channel. If the only available filters are too broad to isolate a specific buy, the campaign structure is the likely cause.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether each active media line or channel in the current plan has a corresponding Pinnacle campaign with a recognizable name. If every active line maps to a distinct, named Pinnacle campaign and the practitioner can filter reporting to that campaign without exporting data, the check passes. If the Pinnacle campaign list is significantly smaller than the number of active lines, uses generic or mismatched names, or cannot be filtered to isolate a specific channel, the check is a fail recorded with the specific gaps between the media plan and the Pinnacle structure.
evaluate.fallback_message: List the Pinnacle campaign names alongside the corresponding media plan lines above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that the campaign structure in DoubleVerify Pinnacle does not align with {{site_name}}'s media plan. The Pinnacle campaign hierarchy does not reflect the channel or campaign breakdowns used in the buy, so viewability, fraud, and brand safety data can only be reviewed in aggregate. Quality issues that may be concentrated in a specific channel or DSP are not visible in the current structure, and diagnosing them requires manual data extraction rather than in-platform filtering. {{voice_recommend}} restructuring the Pinnacle campaign hierarchy to mirror the active media plan, with one Pinnacle campaign per channel or buy line, using naming conventions that match the DSP or the agency's reporting system.

audit_checks.title: DV measurement is not deployed on all active media, leaving a portion of impressions unmeasured
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When DV measurement does not cover all active channels or buying platforms, the portion of spend that falls outside DV's measurement is invisible in Pinnacle. Viewability, fraud, and brand safety data from unmeasured media cannot be assessed or acted on, so quality problems in those placements go undetected. The aggregate quality score reported in Pinnacle reflects only the measured portion, which may be materially better or worse than the true blended result across all spend.

educate.base:
DoubleVerify measures impressions using two primary methods: tag-based measurement and direct integrations with buying platforms. Tag-based measurement attaches a DV tracking tag to each ad creative, which fires at impression time and records quality signals. Direct integrations, sometimes called server-to-server integrations or integrated metrics, allow Pinnacle to receive measurement data from a DSP or ad server without a tag on every creative, typically through a platform-level activation in the DSP's settings.

Coverage gaps arise when either method is missing for a given channel or placement type. A campaign running on a DSP where the DV integration has not been activated sends no data to Pinnacle. A campaign using a creative format that does not support DV tags, such as certain native or in-app formats, may be measured at a different coverage rate. A programmatic guaranteed deal or a direct publisher buy that was set up without DV tagging runs with no measurement at all.

The Media Rating Council (MRC) accredits third-party measurement providers like DoubleVerify against established standards for viewability and invalid traffic detection. MRC-accredited measurement requires that the measurement vendor can actually observe the impressions it is reporting on. Impressions that are not tagged or connected to an integration fall outside that observation window, and DV cannot make a determination about quality for what it has not seen.

A few signs suggest coverage gaps exist:
  • The total impression volume tracked in Pinnacle is materially lower than the total impressions reported in the DSP or ad server for the same period
  • One or more active DSPs or buying platforms do not appear in the Pinnacle data connections list
  • A specific campaign or format type is visible in the media plan but produces no data in Pinnacle
You may want to compare total measured impressions in Pinnacle against total served impressions in each active buying platform to estimate the measurement gap.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retailer running a mix of programmatic display, paid social, and retail media, each channel typically requires a separate deployment step. Retail media networks in particular may not support standard DV tagging and may require platform-specific integrations. You may want to confirm that each channel where budget is active has a corresponding measurement connection in Pinnacle.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser using both programmatic and direct publisher buys, direct placements are a frequent coverage gap. Direct publisher deals set up without DV measurement tags run with no quality data. You may want to confirm that insertion orders include a DV tag requirement and that those tags are verified as firing before the campaign is marked live.

learn_more.source_title: MRC Standards and Guidelines: viewability and invalid traffic detection
Open Measurement (OM) SDK: third-party measurement vendor integration standard

educate.learn_more_note: Guidance on activating DV integrated metrics within specific DSPs and on generating and deploying DV measurement tags is available in the DoubleVerify Pinnacle knowledge base, which requires a Pinnacle login. Contact your DoubleVerify account team for platform-specific activation steps.

investigate.intro: Compare total measured impressions in Pinnacle against total served impressions in each active buying platform to identify channels or placements where measurement coverage is incomplete.

dv_pinnacle.label: DoubleVerify Pinnacle
steps: In DV Pinnacle, open the data connections or integration settings and list every DSP or buying platform that has an active connection sending data to Pinnacle.
Cross-reference that list against every DSP, ad server, or direct publisher buy that is active in the current media plan. Note any buying platform that is missing from the Pinnacle connections list.

Pull a Pinnacle report for the most recent 30-day period showing total measured impressions. Then pull the equivalent impression total from each active DSP or ad server for the same period. Calculate the difference between total served and total measured for each channel.

For any campaign or channel where total measured impressions are significantly lower than served impressions, note the gap as a potential coverage problem and check whether a DV tag or integrated metrics activation is missing for that campaign or format.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active buying platform or channel, whether a DV data connection is active in Pinnacle and whether measured impressions are within a reasonable range of served impressions for the period reviewed. A channel with an active connection and a measured impression volume within 10 to 15 percent of served impressions passes. A channel with no Pinnacle connection, or with a measured impression volume materially lower than served impressions without a documented explanation such as a format exclusion, is a fail recorded with the platform name and the impression gap.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record measured impressions from Pinnacle and served impressions from each buying platform above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that DoubleVerify measurement is not deployed across all of {{site_name}}'s active media. One or more buying platforms or campaign types are sending impressions that Pinnacle is not receiving or measuring, creating a gap between total served impressions and total measured impressions. Quality signals for the unmeasured portion, including viewability, fraud, and brand safety, are unavailable, and the aggregate metrics in Pinnacle reflect only the measured share of the buy. {{voice_recommend}} auditing each active buying platform to confirm it has an active DV data connection in Pinnacle, and where a connection is missing, activating the appropriate integrated metrics or deploying DV measurement tags before the next campaign goes live.

audit_checks.title: Data connections from buying platforms to Pinnacle are missing or misconfigured, resulting in incomplete reporting
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A data connection between a DSP and Pinnacle that is missing or misconfigured causes impression data to arrive in Pinnacle at an incorrect campaign mapping, under the wrong advertiser, or not at all. Practitioners then see either a blank in Pinnacle for media that is running, or a discrepancy between what the DSP reports and what Pinnacle shows, without a reliable way to determine which figure is accurate.

educate.base:
DV Pinnacle receives impression-level data from buying platforms through integrations that need to be configured on both sides: inside Pinnacle and inside the DSP or ad server. When a DSP supports integrated metrics with DoubleVerify, the DSP typically requires the practitioner to enable the DV integration in the DSP's measurement settings and to provide a DV advertiser or campaign ID that tells the DSP how to label the data it sends to DV. DV Pinnacle needs a corresponding connection configured on its end that matches the same advertiser or campaign scope.

Misconfiguration happens in a few ways. The DSP may be enabled for DV at the platform level but not activated for a specific advertiser or campaign, so those campaigns do not send data. The campaign IDs used in the DSP may not match the campaign structure set up in Pinnacle, so data arrives but maps to the wrong campaign or gets dropped. The Pinnacle account may have the integration configured for a previous advertiser ID that no longer matches the active account, which is a common issue when an account migrates from one agency to another.

A correctly configured connection shows impression volume in Pinnacle that corresponds to the campaign activity in the DSP, with campaign-level data appearing under the correct Pinnacle campaign. A misconfigured or absent connection shows either no data or a significant and unexplained discrepancy between served and measured impression counts.

A few signs point to a connection problem:
  • Pinnacle shows zero impressions for a campaign period when the DSP confirms activity ran
  • Pinnacle reports impressions under an unrecognized campaign name or under a campaign that was not active in the period
  • The DSP's own measurement reporting shows a different total for DV-verified impressions than Pinnacle reports for the same campaign
You may want to review the active integrations in Pinnacle's connection settings and compare the advertiser and campaign IDs configured there against the IDs active in each DSP.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retailer using multiple DSPs across a retail media network and an open-market programmatic buy, each DSP typically requires its own separate connection configuration. You may want to confirm that the Pinnacle connection for each DSP references the correct advertiser ID and that the campaign IDs used in each DSP are reflected in the Pinnacle campaign structure.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser with campaigns managed across more than one agency or trading desk, the DV Pinnacle account ownership and the campaign IDs in each DSP may not align if the account changed hands or was restructured. You may want to confirm the Pinnacle account IDs in use match the IDs currently active in each buying platform.

learn_more.source_title: MRC Standards and Guidelines: viewability and invalid traffic detection

educate.learn_more_note: Step-by-step instructions for configuring data connections between specific DSPs and DV Pinnacle are available in the DoubleVerify Pinnacle knowledge base, which requires a Pinnacle login. Contact your DoubleVerify account team for connection setup and troubleshooting guidance.

investigate.intro: Review the active data connections in Pinnacle and compare the configured advertiser and campaign IDs against the IDs active in each buying platform.

dv_pinnacle.label: DoubleVerify Pinnacle
steps: In DV Pinnacle, open the integrations or data connections settings and list every DSP or buying platform that has a configured connection. Note the advertiser ID and any campaign IDs associated with each connection.
Log in to each active DSP and locate the DoubleVerify or third-party measurement settings. Confirm the DV advertiser ID configured in the DSP matches the ID shown in Pinnacle for that connection.

Pull a side-by-side comparison of impression volume from the DSP and from Pinnacle for the same campaign and the same date range. A discrepancy larger than what is attributable to reporting latency or format exclusions points to a connection or mapping problem.

For any campaign showing data in Pinnacle under an unexpected campaign name, open that Pinnacle campaign's settings and note which DSP connection and campaign ID it is associated with. Compare that ID against the current active campaigns in the DSP.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active DSP connection, whether the advertiser ID and campaign IDs in Pinnacle match those in the DSP, and whether the impression volume in Pinnacle is within a reasonable range of the DSP's served impression total for the same period. A connection where IDs match and impression volumes are consistent passes. A connection where IDs do not match, where Pinnacle shows zero impressions for an active period, or where the Pinnacle impression count differs from the DSP by more than a small margin attributable to latency is a fail, recorded with the DSP name, the mismatched IDs, and the impression discrepancy.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the Pinnacle connection settings and the DSP impression totals for the same period above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more data connections between {{site_name}}'s buying platforms and DV Pinnacle are missing or misconfigured. Impression data from the affected DSPs is either absent from Pinnacle, mapped to an incorrect campaign, or arriving at a volume that does not match what the DSP reports for the same period. Practitioners relying on Pinnacle for quality oversight are working with an incomplete picture of the account's actual media performance. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the connection settings in Pinnacle for each active DSP, confirming the advertiser and campaign IDs match those currently active in each buying platform, and reconciling impression discrepancies before the next reporting cycle.
